titleStmt.title: Letter from Captain C. Ua Cuinneagain [O'Cunningham] to Mrs Ellen. Daly, 10 August 1923
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: C. Ua Cuinneagain

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

correspAction.placeName: Drumboe Castle, Stranorlar, Co Donegal, Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
P41/18/2
3RD. INFANTRY BATTALION.
DONEGAL COMMAND. HEADQUARTERS,
DRUMBOW CASTLE,
STRANORLORA, 10TH. AUGUST 1923. TO:- MRS. E. DALY,
KNOCKANCOULTEEN,
FARRANFORE,
CO. KERRY A Chara, I have made enquiries regarding the property
of your son, Charlie Daly, (R.I.P) and am to inform
you that I am forwarding by to-day's post a coat,
the only the only article of his which was left here. I understand that nearly all his belongings were handed
over to Fr. McMullin, C.C. Ballybofey, to be forwarded
to you. One of the prisoners here informed me that Jim Lane,
one of his comrades, who had been transferred from
here some time ago, is in possession of your son's coat. (Trench coat) Mise, C.Ua Cuinneagain CAPTAIN.
Adjt. 3rd. Infantry Battalion. P/S
I am forwarding your communication
to Command Headquarters, least any
of his property has been taken there.
C. Ua. C .
